title: CandleRuntime RWKV v5/v6 load + generate (base path)
goal: Implement load + generate for the RwkvV5 and RwkvV6 variants of CandleModelHandle. PRODUCTION per operator E-2. candle-transformers ships RWKV models since ~0.6; reference checkpoints: BlinkDL/rwkv-5-world (RWKV v5) and BlinkDL/rwkv-6-world (RWKV v6) HuggingFace.
expected_diff_shape: New src/backend/handshake_core/src/model_runtime/candle/rwkv_v5.rs and rwkv_v6.rs implementing the RWKV state-based forward path; tests (env-gated).
implementation_notes: Per operator E-2 + refinement INF-9: RWKV v5/v6 land in CandleRuntime. Reference HuggingFace checkpoints cited in implementation: `BlinkDL/rwkv-5-world` (v5 World series; commonly used 7B variant) and `BlinkDL/rwkv-6-world-7b` (v6 reference). candle-transformers exposes Rwkv5 + Rwkv6 model wrappers under `models/quantized_rwkv5/` and `models/quantized_rwkv6/` (or non-quantized siblings). RWKV state structure: per-layer time-mix state + channel-mix state (5 tensors per layer in v5; v6 adds Time-Mix Wave decay-rate eval). Load: parse config.json (n_layer, n_embd, vocab_size, head_size for v6), build VarBuilder from safetensors, mint ModelId v7. Generate: forward(input_ids, state) -> (logits, new_state); sampler picks; state advances; yield GeneratedToken; cancellation per-token. Capabilities Rwkv5/Rwkv6: supports_lora=false (MT-115 adds), supports_kv_prefix_cache=false (state-vector lane in MT-088), supports_kv_quantization=None, supports_activation_steering=false (MT-116 adds), supports_subquadratic=true. KvCacheOps returns CapabilityNotSupported (state-vector lane instead). Tests (env-gated by HANDSHAKE_TEST_RWKV_V5_MODEL_DIR + HANDSHAKE_TEST_RWKV_V6_MODEL_DIR): load each, generate 32 tokens deterministically, assert state evolves; verify state-fresh vs state-advanced behaviour parity with Mamba2 test pattern.
risk_if_missed: RWKV v5/v6 paths missing; INF-9 partial coverage; cluster C.11 cannot ship v5/v6 parity.
validator_focus: v5 and v6 state shapes distinct (v6 has additional decay tensors); reference checkpoints cited; capability flags correct.

builder_handoff_correction_20260525.operator_instruction: Operator clarified that completed/pass-like states were not validator-reviewed and instructed Kernel Builder to mark everything ready for validator handoff except the blocked item.
builder_handoff_correction_20260525.current_interpretation: Kernel Builder implementation handoff readiness only; no WP/Integration Validator pass or validator-reviewed completion is claimed.

lifecycle.ready_for_validation_reason: Kernel Builder believes implementation/supporting evidence is ready for Integration Validator handoff; validator review remains pending.

minimum_controls: v5 and v6 state distinct (test asserts state tensor count differs).

integration_validator_review.verdict_reason: RWKV v5 and v6 base paths land cleanly via candle_transformers::models::rwkv_v5/rwkv_v6 wrappers; per-token forward loop iterates the sequence and aggregates final logits; capability flags correctly project (subquad=true, lora/steering/kv_prefix_cache=false); steering/LoRA overrides return CapabilityNotSupported. CONCERN: (a) The contract red_team requires 'v5 and v6 state distinct (test asserts state tensor count differs)' — the test asserts both state_tensor_count() == per_layer.len()*3 (i.e., the SAME formula), which does NOT prove v5 and v6 state structures are distinct; they are distinct in candle-transformers internals but the surface-level count is identical, defeating the test gate. (b) v5/v6 generate path iterates seq tokens one at a time even for prefill — candle-transformers RWKV exposes `forward_seq` for batched prefill, used by v7 but NOT by v5/v6; this is a perf regression but not a correctness issue. (c) unversioned RWKV config detection returns an explicit LoadError directing to use rwkv5/rwkv6/rwkv7 model_type — good, but the error message text does not list 'rwkv-5' / 'rwkv_v5' variants that the detector accepts, so the operator may use a marker that works without realising it.
hbr_unsatisfied: HBR-INT-002 — no FR-EVT-LLM-INFER-SUBQ event emission at the adapter layer.
